iti112:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti112:2.1: “Monks, the world has been fully awakened to by the Tathāgata.
iti112:2.2: From the world, the Tathāgata is disjoined.
iti112:2.3: The origination of the world has been fully awakened to by the Tathāgata.
iti112:2.4: The origination of the world has, by the Tathāgata, been abandoned.
iti112:2.5: The cessation of the world has been fully awakened to by the Tathāgata.
iti112:2.6: The cessation of the world has, by the Tathāgata, been realized.
iti112:2.7: The path leading to the cessation of the world has been fully awakened to by the Tathāgata.
iti112:2.8: The path leading to the cessation of the world has, by the Tathāgata, been developed.
iti112:3.1:
“Whatever in this world with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās,
in this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans, its royalty & commonfolk,
is seen, heard, sensed, cognized, attained, sought after,
pondered by the intellect, that has been fully awakened to by the Tathāgata.
iti112:3.2: Thus he is called the Tathāgata.
iti112:4.1:
“From the night the Tathāgata fully awakens to the unsurpassed Right Self-awakening
to the night he is totally unbound in the Unbinding property with no fuel remaining,
whatever the Tathāgata has said, spoken, explained is just so (tatha) and not otherwise.
iti112:4.2: Thus he is called the Tathāgata.
iti112:5.1: “The Tathāgata is one who does in line with (tathā) what he teaches, one who teaches in line with what he does.
iti112:5.2: Because he is one who does in line with what he teaches,
iti112:5.3: one who teaches in line with what he does, he is thus called the Tathāgata.
iti112:6.1:
“In this world with its devas, Māras, & Brahmas, in this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans,
its royalty & common people, the Tathāgata is the unconquered conqueror, total seer, the wielder of power.
iti112:6.2: Thus he is called the Tathāgata.”
iti112:7.2: all the world as it really is,
iti112:8.1: Conquering all in all ways, enlightened,
iti112:9.1: He is free of effluent, of trouble, awakened,
iti112:9.3: has attained the ending of action,
iti112:9.4: is released in the destruction of acquisitions.
iti112:10.3: In the world with its devas
iti112:11.2: who have gone to the Buddha for refuge,
iti112:11.4: to the great one, thoroughly mature:
iti112:12.1: ‘Tamed, he’s the best of those who can be tamed;
iti112:12.2: calm, the seer of those who can be calmed;
iti112:12.3: released, supreme among those who can be released;
iti112:12.4: crossed, the foremost of those who can cross.’
iti112:13.2: to the great one, thoroughly mature:
iti112:13.4: there’s no one to compare with you.’
